info.title: Adraa Inbox API
info.description: Public REST API for Adraa Inbox. Assign agents to conversations and send messages from your own systems. Authenticate every request with a workspace API token.

get.description: Returns the workspace and token associated with the API token used to authenticate the request. Use this endpoint to confirm a token works before wiring up an integration.

company.description: The workspace this token belongs to.

token.description: The API token used for this request.

name.description: Token name set when it was created.

get.description: Lists the active agents of the workspace, including the workspace AI agent when one exists. Use this to look up agent IDs for assignment.

post.description:
Adds an agent to the workspace. If no Adraa Inbox account exists for the email, one is created — no invite email is sent; the person signs in at the console with a one-time code sent to that email. If an account already exists, it is added to the workspace with the given role (the existing display name is kept).

Rate limit: 30 requests per minute.

nickname.description: Display name, unique across Adraa Inbox. Generated from the email when omitted. Ignored if the email already has an account.

409.description: Email already a member, or display name taken

alreadyMember.summary: Email already belongs to a workspace member

error.message: An agent with this email is already a member of the workspace
That display name is already in use

get.description: Returns one active agent of the workspace.

patch.description:
Updates an agent's workspace role and/or display name. The role is scoped to this workspace; the display name is the agent's global name across Adraa Inbox. At least one field is required.

The workspace must keep at least one admin, and platform-managed agents (the AI agent) cannot be modified.

Rate limit: 30 requests per minute.

nickname.description: New display name, unique across Adraa Inbox.

400.description: Demoting the last admin, or a platform-managed agent

error.message: The workspace must keep at least one admin
That display name is already in use

delete.description:
Removes an agent from the workspace. Their Adraa Inbox account and message history remain; they simply lose access to this workspace. Re-adding the same email restores access.

The workspace must keep at least one admin, and platform-managed agents (the AI agent) cannot be removed.

Rate limit: 30 requests per minute.

400.description: Removing the last admin, or a platform-managed agent

error.message: The workspace must keep at least one admin

get.description:
Lists everyone who has messaged the workspace, one row per contact, newest activity first. Mirrors the console's Contacts page: the same search, country, channel, assignment, and open-conversation filters, plus facet counts for building filter UIs.

Contacts are created automatically when a customer first messages the workspace — they cannot be created or deleted through the API.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Matches against the contact's name, email, and phone.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: ISO-3166 alpha-2 country code, derived from the contact's phone number. Use the `facets.countries` of an unfiltered request to see which codes are present.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: Only contacts who have a conversation on this channel.

parameters.name: assignment
parameters.description: `assigned` — the contact's latest conversation has an agent; `unassigned` — it does not.

parameters.name: hasOpen
parameters.description: When `true`, only contacts with at least one open conversation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: 1-based page number. Clamped to the last page when out of range.

200.description: One page of contacts plus facet counts

total.description: Contacts matching the filters, across all pages.

facets.description: Counts across the whole directory (unfiltered), for building filter dropdowns.

get.description: Returns a contact's full profile: identity and custom fields, the automation flows that ran for them, and the internal notes agents left about them.

flows.description: Automation flow runs for this contact, newest first (up to 25).

notes.description: Internal agent notes, newest first. Never visible to the customer.

patch.description:
Updates a contact's display name and/or custom fields — useful for enriching contacts from a CRM. Custom fields are merged key by key: provided keys overwrite, a `null` value removes the key, and keys you don't send are kept. At least one of `name` or `customFields` is required.

Rate limit: 30 requests per minute.

customFields.description: Keys to merge into the contact's custom fields (max 50 per request). Values may be strings (max 500 chars), numbers, booleans, or `null` to remove the key.

400.description: Invalid body — neither name nor customFields provided

get.description: Lists the workspace's conversations, most recently active first, with cursor pagination. Filter by status, channel, assigned agent, or contact, and search with `q` the same way console search works (subject, customer name/email, and message bodies).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: `open` — not closed; `closed`; `unassigned` — open and without an assigned agent; `all` — everything.

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: Only conversations assigned to this agent (list agents with `GET /api/v1/agents`).

parameters.name: contactId
parameters.description: Only this contact's conversations (list contacts with `GET /api/v1/contacts`).

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search term. Matches the subject, the contact's name and email, and message bodies.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The `nextCursor` from the previous page. Omit for the first page.

nextCursor.description: Pass as `cursor` to fetch the next page. `null` when this is the last page.

post.description:
Assigns an agent to a conversation. The agent must be an active member of the workspace. Pass `ai` as the `agentId` to assign the workspace's AI agent. Reassigning replaces the current assignment.

Rate limit: 60 requests per minute.

agentId.description: ID of the agent to assign (list agents with `GET /api/v1/agents`), or `ai` to assign the workspace's AI agent.

400.description: Invalid request — unknown agent or invalid body

403.description: Agent belongs to a different workspace

error.message: Agent cannot be assigned to different company conversation

post.description:
Sends a message to a conversation. The message is delivered on the conversation's channel — web chat, WhatsApp, or email — and appears in the conversation thread as sent by **API**. Sending a message does not change the conversation's assignment.

Rate limit: 60 requests per minute.

body.example: Hi! Your order has shipped and should arrive on Friday.

400.description: WhatsApp reply window closed or invalid body

error.message: WhatsApp's 24-hour reply window is closed. Send an approved template to reopen the conversation.

apiToken.description: Workspace API token created in Settings → API. Tokens start with `adraa_`.

agentId.name: agentId
agentId.description: ID of the agent. List agents with `GET /api/v1/agents` to find it.

conversationId.name: conversationId
conversationId.description: ID of the conversation. Visible in the console URL when a conversation is open: `app.inbox.adraa.ai/<workspace>/conversations/<conversationId>`, or list conversations with `GET /api/v1/conversations`.

contactId.name: contactId
contactId.description: ID of the contact. List contacts with `GET /api/v1/contacts` to find it.

Unauthorized.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked API token

AgentNotFound.description: Agent is not an active member of this workspace

error.message: Agent not found in this workspace

ConversationNotFound.description: Conversation does not exist or belongs to another workspace

ContactNotFound.description: Contact does not exist or has never messaged this workspace

id.description: Agent ID — use it for assignment.

role.description: Workspace role. The workspace AI identity is reported as `ai`.

name.description: Display name — the contact's name, or their phone/email when no name is known.

email.description: Email address, when known. `null` for contacts who only messaged via WhatsApp.

countryIso.description: ISO-3166 alpha-2 country code derived from the phone number.

channels.description: Channels this contact has conversations on.

conversationId.description: The contact's most recently active conversation — use it to assign or message them.

assignedAgent.description: Agent assigned to the contact's latest conversation.

firstContactAt.description: When the contact first messaged the workspace.

customFields.description: Free-form key/value data attached to the contact — set by the chat widget or through `PATCH /api/v1/contacts/{contactId}`.

body.example: VIP customer — fast-track any shipping issues.

agent.description: The agent who wrote the note.

id.description: Conversation ID — use it to assign agents or send messages.

subject.description: Subject line, mainly for email conversations.

assignment.description: The assigned agent, or `null` when unassigned.

senderId.description: Sender identifier. Messages sent through the API use `api:<tokenId>`.

body.example: Hi! Your order has shipped and should arrive on Friday.

metadata.description: Channel delivery metadata. For email conversations this includes the From/To addresses and SES message ID.

status.description: Delivery status. `failed` means the message was stored but could not be delivered on the conversation's channel (for example, the workspace has no verified email domain).

attachments.description: Always empty for API-sent messages; attachments are not yet supported.

sender.description: How the sender is displayed in the conversation. API-sent messages show as "API".
